mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate below 3.5% signals a tightening labor market, which may lead to inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE < 3.5%, or t-test p > 0.05 for returns in low UNRATE regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand-side economic momentum, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 for combined GDPC1/HOUST signals, or 20d returns in triggered periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and CPI (CPIAUCSL) signal aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances during high inflation periods, or interaction effect size drops below 0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103 signals strong manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in >60% of instances when INDPRO > 103, or t-test p > 0.05 for returns in high INDPRO regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong labor market, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in >60% of instances despite PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in retail sales (RSAFS) above 700,000 million dollars signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of instances when RSAFS > 700000, or t-test p > 0.05 for returns in high RSAFS regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicate favorable economic and monetary conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 for combined GDPC1/FEDFUNDS signals, or 20d returns in triggered periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
